description_long: This is a two-story stone house with a steep gable roof covered in dark shingles and a prominent brick chimney on one side. The front facade features a single entry door and two windows, all with simple rectangular frames and no decorative trim. The rear of the house has a large, modern glass extension or conservatory, contrasting with the traditional stonework. The side walls are plain, with minimal window openings and a consistent stone texture throughout. The roof is pitched, and the brick chimney adds a classic touch to the otherwise utilitarian design. The overall appearance suggests a blend of traditional and slightly modernized residential architecture, suitable for dense urban neighborhoods.
description: A two-story stone house with a gable roof, brick chimney, and a modern glass extension at the rear. The facade is simple, with rectangular windows and a single entry door.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entry door and two rectangular windows set in a stone facade.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the brick chimney, plain stone wall, and part of the glass extension at the rear.
description_view_2: Rear view dominated by a large glass extension and two upper-story windows above it.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with a plain stone wall and a partial view of the glass extension roof.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front and rear, minimal on sides
attribute_entryway_design: Simple single door with no porch or decorative trim, flush with the stone facade
